This is in the PR 464 attached to this branch:

pub fn mime_from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    match ext.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
        // Images
        "png" => Some("image/png"),
        "jpg" | "jpeg" => Some("image/jpeg"),
        "gif" => Some("image/gif"),
        "webp" => Some("image/webp"),
        "ppm" => Some("image/x-portable-pixmap"),
        // Documents
        "pdf" => Some("application/pdf"),
        "txt" | "text" | "log" => Some("text/plain"),
        "csv" => Some("text/csv"),
        "json" => Some("application/json"),
        "zip" => Some("application/zip"),
        "gz" | "gzip" => Some("application/gzip"),
        "docx" => Some("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document"),
        "xlsx" => Some("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"),
        "pptx" => Some("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation"),
        "xls" => Some("application/vnd.ms-excel"),
        "doc" => Some("application/msword"),
        "html" | "htm" => Some("text/html"),
        "xml" => Some("application/xml"),
        "rtf" => Some("application/rtf"),
        "md" | "markdown" => Some("text/markdown"),
        // Audio / Video
        "ogg" | "oga" => Some("audio/ogg"),
        "mp3" => Some("audio/mpeg"),
        "mp4" => Some("video/mp4"),
        "webm" => Some("audio/webm"),
        _ => None,
    }
}

I'm pretty sure an existing crate include that instead of doing it manually, can you find one?

content.text:
Yes — **`mime_guess`** is the go-to crate. Usage:

```rust
mime_guess::from_ext("pdf").first_raw()  // Some("application/pdf")
```

It returns `Option<&'static str>` via `.first_raw()`, which is a drop-in replacement for your `mime_from_extension` signature.

Both `mime_from_extension` and `extension_for_mime` can be replaced:
- `mime_from_extension(ext)` → `mime_guess::from_ext(ext).first_raw()`
- `extension_for_mime(mime)` → `mime_guess::get_mime_extensions_str(mime).and_then(|exts| exts.first())`

The `extension_for_mime` reverse direction is slightly less clean since `mime_guess` returns a slice of possible extensions, but it works. You could also keep the reverse mapping manual if you want control over preferred extensions (e.g. "jpg" vs "jpeg").

One caveat: `mime_guess` won't know `"text"` or `"log"` as extensions for `text/plain`, and `"ppm"` may not be in its database. You could handle those few edge cases with a fallback. Want me to make the change?
Let me check the media crate's Cargo.toml and the workspace dependencies first.

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `send_document` tool that lets the LLM send local files (PDF, CSV, DOCX, ZIP, etc.) to conversation channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack). It introduces a clean two-path design: a new "media-ref" path that saves files to the session store and returns a lightweight path reference, and a legacy data-URI fallback for environments without a session store. The PR also extracts shared `file_io` helpers, consolidates MIME maps into `moltis_media::mime`, adds `filename` to `MediaAttachment`, implements `Content-Disposition` for the media API, and auto-detects podman vs. docker via a new `container_cli()` helper. **Key issues found:** - **`container_cli()` blocks an async worker on first call** — the `OnceLock` initializer calls `is_cli_available("podman")`, which runs `std::process::Command::new("podman").arg("--version").status()` (blocking I/O). `container_cli()` is called from `DockerImageBuilder::new()` (used in async startup paths) and directly inside the async `api_check_packages_handler`. The first invocation will stall a Tokio worker thread; `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` or eager sync initialization before the runtime starts is needed. - **Dead `None` branch in `document_payload_from_ref`** — `str::rsplit('/').next()` always returns `Some(…)`; the `None` arm is unreachable, and an empty `ref_filename` produced by an edge-case `media_ref` value will silently pass through to `store.read_media`. - **Non-ASCII filenames produce invalid `Content-Disposition` headers** — `media_content_disposition` lets raw Unicode through into the quoted-string value. RFC 8187 requires `filename*=UTF-8''` for non-ASCII filenames; omitting this will produce malformed headers for non-Latin filenames. - **Audio/video MIME types silently pass the extension filter** — `mime_from_extension` (the full shared map) recognises `.mp3`, `.mp4`, `.ogg`, `.webm`, etc. The tool only rejects `image/` MIMEs; audio/video types slip through, but are not mentioned in the description or schema. Either block them explicitly or document the support. - **`file_name().unwrap_or("document.bin")` hides `..`-path edge cases** — a path ending in `..` returns `None` from `file_name()`; silently falling back to `"document.bin"` is surprising and may interact oddly with the downstream file read. An explicit error is cleaner.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- PR introduces useful functionality with good test coverage, but has a blocking-I/O-in-async issue, a dead code bug, and an RFC compliance gap in Content-Disposition that should be addressed before merging. - The overall design is sound and the full-suite tests are still pending (noted in the PR). The blocking `container_cli()` init could stall Tokio workers in production, the `rsplit` dead-code hides a real empty-filename edge case, and the non-ASCII Content-Disposition issue would silently produce broken downloads for internationalized filenames. None are showstoppers on their own, but together they warrant fixes before merge. - `crates/tools/src/sandbox/containers.rs` (blocking init), `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` (dead None arm), `crates/web/src/api.rs` (RFC 8187 compliance), `crates/tools/src/send_document.rs` (audio/video + file_name edge case)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | crates/tools/src/send_document.rs | New `send_document` tool with extension-based MIME detection, blocked-extensions list, sandbox routing, and session-store media-ref path. Two issues: audio/video MIMEs from the shared map are silently allowed through, and `file_name().unwrap_or("document.bin")` hides `..`-path edge cases. | | crates/tools/src/file_io.rs | Shared file-read module extracted from `send_image`; TOCTOU guard, size validation, sandbox routing, and shell-quoting are all correct. Well tested. | | crates/tools/src/sandbox/containers.rs | New `container_cli()` uses `OnceLock<&str>` with `is_cli_available` (blocking `std::process::Command`) as the init function. When first invoked from an async handler or during async startup, this blocks a Tokio worker thread. | | crates/chat/src/lib.rs | Document dispatch wiring looks correct; new/legacy paths are cleanly separated. The `rsplit('/').next()` None arm in `document_payload_from_ref` is unreachable dead code and misses the empty-filename case. | | crates/web/src/api.rs | `media_content_disposition` is well-structured with good tests, but passes raw Unicode characters directly into the quoted-string header value, which violates RFC 8187 for non-ASCII filenames. | | crates/media/src/mime.rs | Consolidated MIME ↔ extension mapping with solid round-trip tests; clean single source of truth. | | crates/common/src/types.rs | Backward-compatible addition of optional `filename` field to `MediaAttachment` with `serde(default)` and round-trip tests. | | crates/web/src/assets/js/helpers.js | New `renderDocument` helper with icon selection, size formatting, and inline-vs-download logic. Clean implementation following existing patterns. | | crates/tools/src/image_cache.rs | `DockerImageBuilder` now holds `cli: &'static str` from `container_cli()`. Correct podman normalization for `localhost/` prefix. `image_exists` correctly changed from `async fn` to `&self` method. Comments Outside Diff (4)

 1. `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`, line 276-283 ([link](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/ebe769aaca23d0235bddff603525a2d630a86bf5/crates/chat/src/lib.rs#L276-L283)) **`rsplit('/').next()` never returns `None` — dead code branch** `str::rsplit('/').next()` always returns `Some(...)` for any string: for a string with no `/`, it returns the full string; for an empty string, it returns `Some("")`. The `None =>` arm is unreachable, making the warning and early return dead code, and silently passing through an empty `ref_filename` if `media_ref` is `""` or a bare filename with no slash. Add an explicit check for the empty-filename case instead: ```rust let ref_filename = media_ref.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or_default(); if ref_filename.is_empty() { warn!(media_ref, "invalid document_ref path: empty filename"); return None; } ``` 2. `crates/web/src/api.rs`, line 1954-1959 ([link](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/ebe769aaca23d0235bddff603525a2d630a86bf5/crates/web/src/api.rs#L1954-L1959)) **Non-ASCII filenames produce invalid `Content-Disposition` headers** The sanitization strips `"`, `\n`, `\r`, `;`, and `\`, but passes Unicode/non-ASCII characters through directly into the quoted-string parameter value. RFC 7230 §3.2.6 requires header field values to contain only visible ASCII (0x21–0x7E) plus SP and HTAB; raw multi-byte UTF-8 in a header value is not standards-conformant. RFC 8187 requires non-ASCII filenames to use the `filename*=UTF-8''` extended syntax. A user uploading `Отчёт.pdf` (Cyrillic) or `报告.pdf` (CJK) will generate a malformed header that many HTTP clients and CDNs will silently corrupt or reject. Consider using the extended notation when the filename contains non-ASCII characters: ```rust let has_non_ascii = safe_name.chars().any(|c| !c.is_ascii()); if has_non_ascii { let encoded = percent_encoding::utf8_percent_encode(&safe_name, percent_encoding::NON_ALPHANUMERIC); format!("{disposition}; filename=\"{safe_name}\"; filename*=UTF-8''{encoded}") } else { format!("{disposition}; filename=\"{safe_name}\"") } ``` 3. `crates/tools/src/send_document.rs`, line 1217-1230 ([link](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/ebe769aaca23d0235bddff603525a2d630a86bf5/crates/tools/src/send_document.rs#L1217-L1230)) **`mime_from_extension` rejects audio/video extensions with a misleading error** `send_document` calls `mime_from_extension` (the full media MIME map from `moltis_media`) and then rejects MIME types starting with `"image/"` — but `mime_from_extension` also maps `.mp3` → `audio/mpeg`, `.mp4` → `video/mp4`, `.ogg` → `audio/ogg`, etc. Passing `/tmp/recording.mp3` will reach `mime_from_extension` and return `Some("audio/mpeg")`, which is not blocked. The user will then receive an "unsupported file extension" message only for truly unknown extensions. If audio/video files are intentionally out of scope for `send_document`, explicitly reject them with a clear message (mirroring the `send_image` redirect): ```rust if mime.starts_with("audio/") || mime.starts_with("video/") { return Err(Error::message( "use the dedicated audio/video tool for media files", ).into()); } ``` If they are intentionally allowed (the MIME map covers them), update `description()` to mention audio/video support. 4. `crates/tools/src/send_document.rs`, line 1240-1244 ([link](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/ebe769aaca23d0235bddff603525a2d630a86bf5/crates/tools/src/send_document.rs#L1240-L1244)) **`unwrap_or` on `file_name()` silently hides path-without-filename cases** `file_path.file_name()` returns `None` if the path ends with `..` (e.g., `"/tmp/reports/.."`). Falling back to `"document.bin"` silently produces a misleading filename and continues, whereas the file read itself will succeed (reading the parent directory as a "file") or fail with a confusing error from `read_file_for_session`. Return an explicit error instead: ```rust let filename = file_path .file_name() .and_then(|n| n.to_str()) .ok_or_else(|| Error::message("path must point to a file, not a directory component"))? .to_string(); ```
